L. 110–199, title I, § 103(b)122 Stat. 668Apr. 9, 2008“Notwithstanding section 2952(2) of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 () [now ], each grantee under part EE of such Act [ et seq.] shall have not more than 3 years from the date of the enactment of this Act [] to adopt the definition of ‘violent offender’ under such part, as amended by subsection (a) of this section [amending this section].”42 U.S.C. 3797u–1(2)34 U.S.C. 10612(2)34 U.S.C. 10611Apr. 9, 2008

children.label: A
children.text: the person carried, possessed, or used a firearm or dangerous weapon;

children.label: B
children.text: there occurred the death of or serious bodily injury to any person; or

children.label: C
children.text: there occurred the use of force against the person of another, without regard to whether any of the circumstances described in subparagraph (A) or (B) is an element of the offense or conduct of which or for which the person is charged or convicted; or
has 1 or more prior convictions for a felony crime of violence involving the use or attempted use of force against a person with the intent to cause death or serious bodily harm.

children.heading: Definition for purposes of juvenile drug courts
children.label: b Definition for purposes of juvenile drug courts

children.text: has as an element, the use, attempted use, or threatened use of physical force against the person or property of another, or the possession or use of a firearm; or
by its nature, involves a substantial risk that physical force against the person or property of another may be used in the course of committing the offense.
